description: Natural-language user requests that avoid direct skill names and obvious retrieval keywords. These cases test whether the LLM can rewrite intent into a useful query_plan before deterministic compact-card retrieval.

cases.user_query: Build a block that accepts bytes from upstream only when it can take them, then drives a single idle-high line with a start marker, the data bits, and an end marker. Expose whether a frame is currently in progress.
cases.notes: Avoids saying UART, TX, transmitter, ready, valid, or busy directly.

cases.user_query: I need elastic storage between a streaming producer and consumer. It should absorb bursts, preserve order, and tell each side when transfer can continue.
cases.notes: Avoids saying AXI, FIFO, valid, ready, full, or empty directly.

cases.user_query: Several input lines may be asserted at once. Pick the winning line according to a fixed precedence order and also return its numeric position.
When the downstream side pauses for a cycle, keep one transfer stable so no item is dropped or duplicated once the downstream side resumes.
cases.notes: Avoids saying AXI-stream register, ready/valid, skid, or buffer directly.

cases.user_query: Connect a streaming producer and consumer that use different chunk sizes while preserving the backpressure handshake and byte ordering.
cases.notes: Avoids saying AXI-stream, adapter, or width conversion directly.

cases.user_query: Create combinational logic that updates a 32-bit polynomial residue over incoming data so software can compare a packet integrity value later.
cases.notes: Targets OpenTitan built skills under work/built_skills.

cases.user_query: Given a compact binary number and an enable, assert exactly one output line corresponding to that number.
cases.notes: Targets OpenTitan built skills while avoiding the exact onehot encoder name.

cases.user_query: Provide a small hardware tally that can increment on events, be read by software, and be cleared or written through control logic.
cases.notes: Targets Ibex built skills while avoiding the word counter.
